req.title: System-Admin creates onboarding-task
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to create a onboarding-task through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
Here's a comprehensive pytest test suite for the onboarding-task creation requirement:

```python
"""
Tests for: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to create an onboarding-task
through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must
be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def system_admin_user():
    """A fully authenticated system-admin user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "user-001"
    user.username = "admin@example.com"
    user.role = "system-admin"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_admin_user():
    """An authenticated user without system-admin role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "user-002"
    user.username = "regular@example.com"
    user.role = "viewer"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """An unauthenticated (anonymous) user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.username = None
    user.role = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def valid_task_payload():
    """Minimal valid payload containing all mandatory fields."""
    return {
        "title": "Complete HR orientation",
        "description": "New hire must attend the HR orientation session.",
        "due_days": 5,
        "assigned_role": "new-hire",
        "category": "hr",
    }


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit_service():
    with patch("app.services.audit_service.write_audit_record") as mock_audit:
        yield mock_audit


@pytest.fixture
def mock_task_repository():
    with patch("app.repositories.onboarding_task_repository.create") as mock_repo:
        mock_repo.return_value = MagicMock(
            id="task-999",
            title="Complete HR orientation",
            created_at=datetime(2024, 1, 15, 10, 0, 0),
        )
        yield mock_repo


@pytest.fixture
def onboarding_task_service(mock_task_repository, mock_audit_service):
    """
    Provides the service under test with its dependencies replaced by mocks.
    Import path should match your actual project structure.
    """
    from app.services.onboarding_task_service import OnboardingTaskService

    return OnboardingTaskService(
        task_repository=mock_task_repository,
        audit_service=mock_audit_service,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper – simulates a UI form submission going through the service layer
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def submit_create_form(service, user, payload):
    """
    Simulate the action triggered by the 'Save' button on the UI form.
    Returns whatever the service layer returns (task object or raises).
    """
    return service.create_onboarding_task(actor=user, data=payload)


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Authentication & Authorisation
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuthenticationAndAuthorisation:

    def test_system_admin_can_access_create_form(self, system_admin_user):
        """System-admin should be allowed to open the creation form."""
        from app.forms.onboarding_task_form import OnboardingTaskForm
        from app.permissions import can_create_onboarding_task

        assert can_create_onboarding_task(system_admin_user) is True

    def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_task(
        self, onboarding_task_service, unauthenticated_user, valid_task_payload
    ):
        """Unauthenticated users must be rejected with an AuthenticationError."""
        from app.exceptions import AuthenticationError

        with pytest.raises(AuthenticationError):
            submit_create_form(
                onboarding_task_service, unauthenticated_user, valid_task_payload
            )

    def test_non_admin_role_cannot_create_task(
        self, onboarding_task_service, non_admin_user, valid_task_payload
    ):
        """Users without system-admin role must be rejected with an AuthorisationError."""
        from app.exceptions import AuthorisationError

        with pytest.raises(AuthorisationError):
            submit_create_form(
                onboarding_task_service, non_admin_user, valid_task_payload
            )

    def test_system_admin_is_not_blocked_by_permission_check(
        self, onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
    ):
        """System-admin should pass the permission gate without raising."""
        # If any auth/authz exception is raised the test fails automatically.
        submit_create_form(
            onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
        )


# ===========================================================================
# 2. Mandatory Field Validation
# ===========================================================================

MANDATORY_FIELDS = ["title", "description", "due_days", "assigned_role", "category"]


class TestMandatoryFieldValidation:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_raises_validation_error(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
        missing_field,
    ):
        """Each mandatory field, when absent, must trigger a ValidationError."""
        from app.exceptions import ValidationError

        payload = dict(valid_task_payload)
        del payload[missing_field]

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError) as exc_info:
            submit_create_form(onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, payload)

        assert missing_field in str(exc_info.value).lower(), (
            f"ValidationError should mention the missing field '{missing_field}'"
        )

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_empty_string_mandatory_field_raises_validation_error(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
        missing_field,
    ):
        """Blank strings for mandatory fields must also fail validation."""
        from app.exceptions import ValidationError

        payload = dict(valid_task_payload)
        payload[missing_field] = ""

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            submit_create_form(onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, payload)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_none_mandatory_field_raises_validation_error(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
        missing_field,
    ):
        """None values for mandatory fields must fail validation."""
        from app.exceptions import ValidationError

        payload = dict(valid_task_payload)
        payload[missing_field] = None

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            submit_create_form(onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, payload)

    def test_all_mandatory_fields_present_does_not_raise_validation_error(
        self, onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
    ):
        """A payload with all mandatory fields must pass validation."""
        # No exception expected; implicitly asserts on no ValidationError.
        submit_create_form(onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload)

    def test_validation_error_contains_all_missing_fields_when_multiple_absent(
        self, onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user
    ):
        """When several mandatory fields are missing, all should be reported at once."""
        from app.exceptions import ValidationError

        payload = {}  # completely empty submission

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError) as exc_info:
            submit_create_form(onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, payload)

        error_message = str(exc_info.value).lower()
        for field in MANDATORY_FIELDS:
            assert field in error_message, (
                f"Expected '{field}' to appear in the ValidationError message"
            )

    def test_due_days_must_be_positive_integer(
        self, onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
    ):
        """due_days = 0 or negative must be rejected as invalid."""
        from app.exceptions import ValidationError

        for invalid_value in [0, -1, -100]:
            payload = dict(valid_task_payload)
            payload["due_days"] = invalid_value

            with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="due_days"):
                submit_create_form(
                    onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, payload
                )

    def test_due_days_non_integer_raises_validation_error(
        self, onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
    ):
        """Non-integer due_days must fail validation."""
        from app.exceptions import ValidationError

        payload = dict(valid_task_payload)
        payload["due_days"] = "five"

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="due_days"):
            submit_create_form(onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, payload)

    def test_title_exceeding_max_length_raises_validation_error(
        self, onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
    ):
        """Title longer than the allowed maximum must fail validation."""
        from app.exceptions import ValidationError

        payload = dict(valid_task_payload)
        payload["title"] = "A" * 256  # adjust limit to match your domain rules

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="title"):
            submit_create_form(onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, payload)

    def test_invalid_category_value_raises_validation_error(
        self, onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
    ):
        """Category must be one of the allowed enum values."""
        from app.exceptions import ValidationError

        payload = dict(valid_task_payload)
        payload["category"] = "not-a-real-category"

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="category"):
            submit_create_form(onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, payload)


# ===========================================================================
# 3. Successful Creation
# ===========================================================================

class TestSuccessfulTaskCreation:

    def test_task_is_persisted_on_valid_submission(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
        mock_task_repository,
    ):
        """Repository create() must be called exactly once with the submitted data."""
        submit_create_form(
            onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
        )

        mock_task_repository.assert_called_once()
        call_kwargs = mock_task_repository.call_args[1]  # keyword args
        assert call_kwargs.get("title") == valid_task_payload["title"]

    def test_returned_object_contains_generated_id(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
    ):
        """The service must return the newly created task with a server-generated ID."""
        task = submit_create_form(
            onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
        )

        assert task.id is not None
        assert task.id != ""

    def test_returned_task_reflects_submitted_title(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
    ):
        """The returned task title must match the submitted value."""
        task = submit_create_form(
            onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
        )

        assert task.title == valid_task_payload["title"]

    def test_created_at_timestamp_is_set(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
    ):
        """The returned task must include a created_at timestamp."""
        task = submit_create_form(
            onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
        )

        assert task.created_at is not None
        assert isinstance(task.created_at, datetime)


# ===========================================================================
# 4. Audit Record
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditRecord:

    def test_audit_record_written_on_successful_creation(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """An audit record must be written when a task is created successfully."""
        submit_create_form(
            onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
        )

        mock_audit_service.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_contains_actor_id(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Audit record must reference the ID of the acting system-admin."""
        submit_create_form(
            onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
        )

        _, audit_kwargs = mock_audit_service.call_args
        assert audit_kwargs.get("actor_id") == system_admin_user.id

    def test_audit_record_contains_action_type(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Audit record must record the action type as 'CREATE_ONBOARDING_TASK'."""
        submit_create_form(
            onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
        )

        _, audit_kwargs = mock_audit_service.call_args
        assert audit_kwargs.get("action") == "CREATE_ONBOARDING_TASK"

    def test_audit_record_contains_new_task_id(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Audit record must reference the ID of the newly created task."""
        submit_create_form(
            onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
        )

        _, audit_kwargs = mock_audit_service.call_args
        assert audit_kwargs.get("resource_id") == "task-999"

    def test_audit_record_contains_timestamp(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Audit record must include a timestamp."""
        submit_create_form(
            onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
        )

        _, audit_kwargs = mock_audit_service.call_args
        assert audit_kwargs.get("timestamp") is not None

    def test_no_audit_record_written_when_validation_fails(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Audit must NOT be written when validation rejects the submission."""
        from app.exceptions import ValidationError

        payload = dict(valid_task_payload)
        del payload["title"]

        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            submit_create_form(onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, payload)

        mock_audit_service.assert_not_called()

    def test_no_audit_record_written_when_authorisation_fails(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        non_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Audit must NOT be written when an unauthorised user attempts creation."""
        from app.exceptions import AuthorisationError

        with pytest.raises(AuthorisationError):
            submit_create_form(
                onboarding_task_service, non_admin_user, valid_task_payload
            )

        mock_audit_service.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_record_written_exactly_once_not_twice(
        self,
        onboarding_task_service,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """Audit service must be called exactly once per successful creation."""
        submit_create_form(
            onboarding_task_service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload
        )

        assert mock_audit_service.call_count == 1


# ===========================================================================
# 5. Repository / Persistence Failure Handling
# ===========================================================================

class TestPersistenceFailureHandling:

    def test_audit_not_written_when_persistence_fails(
        self,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """If the repository raises an error, no audit record should be written."""
        from app.exceptions import PersistenceError
        from app.services.onboarding_task_service import OnboardingTaskService

        failing_repo = MagicMock(side_effect=PersistenceError("DB unavailable"))
        service = OnboardingTaskService(
            task_repository=failing_repo,
            audit_service=mock_audit_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises(PersistenceError):
            submit_create_form(service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload)

        mock_audit_service.assert_not_called()

    def test_persistence_failure_propagates_as_service_error(
        self,
        system_admin_user,
        valid_task_payload,
        mock_audit_service,
    ):
        """An infrastructure failure must surface as a recognisable service error."""
        from app.exceptions import PersistenceError
        from app.services.onboarding_task_service import OnboardingTaskService

        failing_repo = MagicMock(side_effect=PersistenceError("DB unavailable"))
        service = OnboardingTaskService(
            task_repository=failing_repo,
            audit_service=mock_audit_service,
        )

        with pytest.raises((PersistenceError, Exception)):
            submit_create_form(service, system_admin_user, valid_task_payload)
```

---

## Key design decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|
| **Grouped into 5 classes** | Maps directly to distinct requirement concerns: auth, validation, success, audit, and failure modes |
| **`@pytest.mark.parametrize` over MANDATORY_FIELDS** | Each field gets its own test run; a regression on one field doesn't hide failures on others |
| **Mocked repository & audit service** | Keeps tests fast and deterministic; no real DB or I/O needed |
| **Negative audit tests** | Explicitly verifies audit is *not* written on validation or auth failure — a common omission in real implementations |
| **`submit_create_form` helper** | Single integration point; if the service method signature changes, only one line needs updating |
| **Import paths inside test bodies** | Allows the file to be parsed even before `app` exists; swap with top-level imports once your structure is finalised |

    return user

    return {
        "title": "Complete HR orientation",
        "description": "New hire must attend the HR orientation session.",

        "assigned_role": "new-hire",
        "category": "hr",

@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit_service():

        yield mock_audit


@pytest.fixture
def mock_task_repository():

            id="task-999",
            title="Complete HR orientation",
            created_at=datetime(2024, 1, 15, 10, 0, 0),
        )
        yield mock_repo


@pytest.fixture
def onboarding_task_service(mock_task_repository, mock_audit_service):
    """
    Provides the service under test with its dependencies replaced by mocks.
    Import path should match your actual project structure.
    """
    from app.services.onboarding_task_service import OnboardingTaskService

    return OnboardingTaskService(
        task_repository=mock_task_repository,
        audit_service=mock_audit_service,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper – simulates a UI form submission going through the service layer
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def submit_create_form(service, user, payload):

    Simulate the action triggered by the 'Save' button on the UI form.

    """

# ===========================================================================
# 1. Authentication & Authorisation
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuthenticationAndAuthorisation:

    def test_system_admin_can_access_create_form(self, system_admin_user):
        """System-admin should be allowed to open the creation form."""
        from app.forms.onboarding_task_form import OnboardingTaskForm

        self, onboarding_task_service, unauthenticated_user, valid_task_payload
    ):
        """Unauthenticated users must be rejected with an AuthenticationError."""
        from app.exceptions import AuthenticationError

        with pytest.raises(AuthenticationError):
